title: i18n scaffolding (per-language-review-gated; no machine-translated legal claims shipped)
context: Know-Your-Rights will eventually serve non-English speakers, who are disproportionately among those who need access-to-justice resources. However, machine-translating legal claims is dangerous — errors in translation can mislead people in high-stakes situations. This task scaffolds the i18n infrastructure so that translated content can be added later, with each language gated on a combined legal + linguistic review. No translated legal claims are produced or shipped in this task. Depends on the static site (task 012).
objective: Add i18n scaffolding to the static-site and content pipeline so that translated content can be added per-language in future tasks, each gated on a combined legal review (licensed attorney in the jurisdiction) and linguistic review (native-speaker reviewer). The scaffolding must make the per-language review gate a structural property of the build, not an optional step.
acceptanceCriteria: i18n infrastructure added to the static-site app: locale routing, language selector UI, and a mechanism for per-language content files
The English (en) content from tasks 009-010 renders correctly through the i18n infrastructure with no regressions
A placeholder for a second language (e.g., es or zh) is added to demonstrate the scaffolding without shipping any translated legal claims

The build system enforces that any non-English language directory requires a review-gate marker file (e.g., .reviewed-legal + .reviewed-linguistic) before its content is included in the build output; a language directory without both markers fails the build

Documentation added explaining the per-language review requirement: each translation is a HIGH-tier task requiring licensed-attorney review (in the jurisdiction) plus native-speaker linguistic review before any translated legal claim ships
pnpm build && pnpm test pass with the i18n scaffolding wired in
output: A merged PR adding i18n scaffolding to the publishing pipeline: locale routing, language selector, per-language content directories, and a build gate enforcing that translations require combined legal + linguistic review markers before being included in the build.
